title: 10 best herbal creams for anxiety

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with anxiety because they harness the calming properties of passionflower, a natural herb known for its anxiolytic effects. The cream's active ingredients work to soothe the nervous system, reducing feelings of tension and stress that often accompany anxiety. Additionally, maypop's gentle sedative properties can help quiet a racing mind and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to better manage their emotions and find calm in chaotic situations.
remedy_properties: Passionflower: Maypop creams containing passionflower extract have been shown to help alleviate anxiety by acting as a mild sedative and calming agent, reducing symptoms such as restlessness and agitation.

Valerian root: Valerian root in maypop creams has a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety symptoms like racing thoughts and rapid heartbeat by promoting relaxation and calmness.
Lemon balm: Lemon balm in maypop creams has a mild sedative and calming effect, which can help alleviate anxiety by reducing stress levels, improving mood, and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for reducing anxiety.
Fruits: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have a soothing and relaxing effect on the mind and body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers in early morning dew.
Combine the dried flowers with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the infused oil and heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with anxiety because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce feelings of stress and unease. The creamy texture of the cream allows for easy absorption into the skin, releasing a gentle and continuous stream of lavender essential oils that promote relaxation and tranquility. This can be especially beneficial for individuals who struggle with anxiety-induced insomnia or restlessness, as it provides a natural and calming way to unwind before bed.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Lavender oil's sedative and calming properties can help to reduce anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities: Lavender oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to anxiety disorders, and also protect against oxidative stress caused by anxiety.

GABA-boosting effects: The linalool content in lavender oil can interact with GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, thereby promoting feelings of calmness and reducing anxiety symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are used for their calming and sedative properties, which can help soothe anxiety symptoms.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain a high concentration of linalool, an essential oil that has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavender flowers lavandula angustifolia and sterilized equipment for preparation.
Combine one cup of dried lavender flowers with two cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to facilitate infusion of lavender oil into the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the lavender solids and discard the solids.
Mix one part of the infused oil with three parts of beeswax in a double boiler heating until melted and smooth then pour into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to promote relaxation and calmness. The cream is absorbed through the skin, directly affecting the brain's neurotransmitters, which are responsible for regulating mood and emotions. Valerian root has been shown to interact with GABA receptors, producing a sedative effect that soothes an anxious mind. As a result, individuals using valerian creams often report reduced symptoms of anxiety, such as restlessness and irritability, allowing them to feel more centered and composed throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Helps to reduce anxiety by inhibiting the breakdown of GABA, a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and calmness, thereby increasing its levels in the brain.
Isovaleric acid: Possesses sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms such as restlessness and irritability.

Furoic acids: Exhibits a relaxing effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress by increasing the activity of neurotransmitters that promote relaxation, such as GABA and serotonin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative properties, making it an effective ingredient for calming anxious minds.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes and valepotriates, which have been shown to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and wash them thoroughly in cold running water for 15 minutes.
Dry the washed roots in a warm oven at 60 degrees celsius for 3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix the powdered valeriana officinalis with 200ml of melted coconut oil and 100ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to sterilized jars and storing it in a dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with anxiety because it contains Adiantum capillus-veneris, a natural extract that has been used for centuries to calm the mind and soothe the nerves. The cream's active compounds work to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of tension and unease. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate physical symptoms of anxiety such as headaches and insomnia, making it a natural and effective solution for managing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair fern contains antioxidants that can help protect against oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to anxiety disorders.

GABA-enhancing compounds: Some studies suggest that extracts from Maidenhair fern may enhance the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate calmness and relaxation.
Adaptogenic properties: Maidenhair fern is believed to possess adaptogenic properties, meaning it may help the body adapt to stress and promote feelings of balance and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties and can be used to calm the nervous system.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-anxiety effects by regulating neurotransmitters and reducing stress levels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves in a dark and cool place for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 40 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the coconut oil with the ginkgo biloba extract.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil to the infused oil and stir until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with anxiety because they contain rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has a calming effect on the mind and body. The cream is easily absorbed through the skin, allowing the active ingredients to work quickly and efficiently to soothe frayed nerves. As it calms the nervous system, lemon balm cream can help reduce feelings of stress and anxiety, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility. With regular use, individuals may find themselves better equipped to handle anxious situations with ease and confidence.
remedy_properties: Essential oils: The essential oils present in lemon balm creams, such as citral and geraniol, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety symptoms.

Adaptogenic compounds: Lemon balm contains adaptogenic compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help the body adapt to stress and promote relaxation by reducing cortisol levels and promoting GABA activity.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in lemon balm creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to anxiety disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are rich in calcium and magnesium, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers of high quality.
Grind the herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered herbs with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat stirring occasionally for 30 minutes to melt beeswax.
Fill sterilized jars with cooled cream leaving headspace for proper sealing and storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime creams helps with anxiety because they contain a unique combination of natural herbs and citrus essential oils that work together to calm the mind and body. The calming properties of herbs like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender help to reduce feelings of stress and anxiety, while the invigorating scent of lime juice stimulates the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate mood. As a result, individuals who consume herbal lime creams may experience a sense of relaxation and tranquility, making it easier to manage anxious thoughts and emotions.
remedy_properties: GABA-enhancing properties: The herbs present in herbal lime creams, such as lavender and chamomile, contain compounds that boost GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which helps to calm anxiety and promote relaxation.

Adaptogenic effects: Herbs like ashwagandha and lemon balm in herbal lime creams have adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stressors, reducing feelings of anxiety and promoting emotional balance.

Sedative and anti-inflammatory compounds: The flavonoids and terpenes present in herbs like lemongrass and bergamot in herbal lime creams can help reduce inflammation in the brain, leading to a sense of calmness and relaxation, and may also have sedative effects to help alleviate anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lime plant have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties, which help reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of the lime plant contain compounds that have a sedative effect, making them effective in reducing anxiety symptoms such as restlessness and agitation.

Barks: The barks of the lime plant are used to make medicinal creams for anxiety because they possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce anxiety-induced inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia x europaea flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator at 55 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Combine one part dried tilia x europaea flowers with two parts coconut oil and infuse for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove solids and transfer to a double boiler.
Heat the infusion to 70 degrees celsius while stirring constantly for 30 minutes then let cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with anxiety because they contain a unique combination of natural compounds that promote relaxation and calmness. The alpha-acid content in hops has been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and promoting feelings of serenity. Additionally, hops have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stress and tension, leading to a sense of balance and well-being. Regular application of herbal hops creams can provide long-lasting relief from anxiety symptoms, allowing individuals to feel more centered and at ease.
remedy_properties: Tryptophan content: Herbal hops creams contain tryptophan, an amino acid that is converted into serotonin in the brain, helping to regulate mood and reduce anxiety symptoms.
Humulone: Humulone, a bitter acid found in hops, has been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by increasing GABA levels in the brain, which helps to calm nervous activity.

Xanthohumol: Xanthohumol is a flavonoid compound found in hops that has been demonstrated to have anti-anxiety properties by inhibiting the breakdown of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, leading to improved mood regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect and can help alleviate anxiety symptoms when used in topical creams.
Buds: Hops buds are rich in lupulone, a compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic properties and can help reduce feelings of nervousness and restlessness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus flowers and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil for infusion.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan and heat at low temperature for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass container.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 1/4 cup of jojoba oil in a double boiler with 2 ounces of the infused coconut oil.
Remove from heat after 10 minutes and let it cool and solidify before transferring to a glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams help with anxiety because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic properties. When applied topically, it is absorbed into the skin and transported to the brain, where it can interact with neurotransmitters to reduce feelings of stress and worry. This natural ingredient can also help regulate serotonin levels, which are often imbalanced in individuals with anxiety disorders. By promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of anxiety, St John's wort creams offer a soothing and effective solution for managing anxiety.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has been shown to inhibit the reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine in the brain, increasing their availability and helping to reduce symptoms of anxiety.

Hypotensive activity: St. John's Wort creams have been found to dilate blood vessels, which can help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate feelings of tension and anxiety associated with hypertension.

Anxiolytic properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in St. John's Wort have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of hyperforin, a compound known for its anxiolytic and antidepressant properties.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them ideal for anxiety relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves from pesticide-free areas.
Combine the harvested hypericum perforatum with 1 cup of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes, then allow it to cool to room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and add to the hypericum-infused coconut oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with anxiety because they contain the herb Bacopa monnieri, which has been traditionally used to enhance memory, cognitive function, and mental clarity. The cream absorbs into the skin and delivers the active ingredients directly to the brain, where they can help to calm and soothe the nervous system. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms of anxiety, such as racing thoughts, restlessness, and irritability, allowing individuals to feel more centered and focused.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety by normalizing cortisol levels in the body, promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system.

Neuroprotective and cognitive-enhancing compounds: Brahmi contains bioactive compounds like brahmine, herpestine, and gallic acid that have been shown to improve memory, concentration, and mood, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms such as racing thoughts and mental fogginess.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) flavonoids: The flavonoid content in Brahmi has been found to possess anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce anxiety by modulating neurotransmitters like GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, leading to a sense of calm and reduced feelings of nervousness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, brahmi roots are used to create calming and grounding effects in cream form.
Rhizomes: The underground stem of the brahmi plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anxiolytic properties that help reduce anxiety symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest bacopa monnieri leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier, ensuring they are organic and free of contaminants.
Dry the fresh bacopa monnieri leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or air-drying them for several days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried bacopa monnieri leaves into a fine powder using an herb grinder or mortar and pestle, aiming for a yield of 1:4 ratio to the cream base.
Mix the powdered bacopa monnieri with 2% w/w beeswax and 20% w/w coconut oil in a double boiler, heating at 160°f for 10 minutes to create a smooth paste.
Cool the mixture to room temperature and whip it into a creamy texture using an electric mixer, adding a pinch of vitamin e oil to enhance shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with anxiety because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work synergistically to calm the mind and body. The bitter orange peel, in particular, has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce feelings of stress and unease. Additionally, the creamy texture and subtle flavor of these creams provide a soothing and comforting sensation, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Boosts the body's natural mood-boosting neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Flavonoids: Act as a calming agent by reducing inflammation in the brain and promoting relaxation, helping to mitigate anxiety and stress.
Citrus Limonoids: Have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, potentially due to their ability to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, which can help regulate mood.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and limonene, which have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic properties, making them effective in calming anxiety symptoms.
Barks: Containing a unique combination of alkaloids and glycosides, the barks of bitter orange have been traditionally used to treat anxiety-related disorders due to their sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus x aurantium peels and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for two hours.
Combine one ounce of dried citrus x aurantium peel with four ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Add five drops of lavender essential oil and mix well to create a calming blend.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify before applying it to the skin as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana creams helps with anxiety because they provide a localized, gentle delivery system for cannabis compounds to target specific areas of the body. The topical application allows for a more precise dosage, reducing the risk of psychoactive effects often associated with ingested products. The creams' active ingredients, such as CBD and THC, interact with the body's endocannabinoid system, promoting relaxation and calming sensations that can alleviate anxiety symptoms. As a result, individuals can experience a sense of calm and relief without feeling impaired or high.
remedy_properties: Cannabidiol (CBD): Reduces anxiety by interacting with serotonin receptors in the brain, promoting feelings of calmness and relaxation. CBD has been shown to have anxiolytic effects without producing euphoria or intoxication.

Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC): Relieves anxiety by binding to cannabinoid receptors in the brain, reducing inflammation and stress responses that contribute to anxiety symptoms. THC's psychoactive properties can also help distract from anxious thoughts and emotions.

Terpenes: Such as limonene, linalool, and pinene, which have a calming effect on the nervous system by interacting with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marijuana plant contain rich sources of terpenoids and flavonoids, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Barks: Marijuana barks have been shown to possess anti-anxiety properties due to the presence of alkaloids, which can help calm the nervous system and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cannabis sativa, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried cannabis sativa leaves with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 60 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate decarboxylation and infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and storing the liquid for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with anxiety because they contain a natural compound called baicalin, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. When applied topically to the skin, baicalin is absorbed into the bloodstream and works to reduce stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline. This can lead to a sense of relaxation and reduced feelings of overwhelm, helping individuals better cope with anxiety-induced symptoms such as racing thoughts and an increased heart rate. Regular use of skullcap creams may also promote long-term reductions in anxiety levels.
remedy_properties: Scutellaria flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
Baicalin: A key constituent of skullcap, baicalin has been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxiety and promote better sleep quality.

Scopoletin: This alkaloid has been shown to possess anxiolytic properties by blocking the action of neurotransmitters involved in anxiety, such as serotonin and dopamine, resulting in reduced anxiety symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Skullcap roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties.
Rhyzomes: Skullcap rhyzomes are rich in sesquiterpenes, which are believed to contribute to the plant's calming effects on the nervous system, making them useful for anxiety relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality scutellaria baicalensis roots in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of ground root powder with 100ml of jojoba oil and 50ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 2 hours while stirring occasionally until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and store it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine creams helps with anxiety because they contain a natural extract that interacts with the brain's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and calming the mind. The cream's soothing properties help to reduce stress and anxiety by slowing down an overactive nervous system, allowing individuals to feel more centered and composed. Additionally, the gentle, non-invasive application of the cream allows for a sense of calm to spread throughout the body, providing long-lasting relief from anxious feelings.
remedy_properties: Aphelandra flavescens extract: This extract has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.

GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid): Yellow jasmine creams often contain GABA, a neurotransmitter that helps regulate the activity of nerve cells in the brain. Supplementing with GABA can help promote a sense of calmness and reduce anxiety levels.

Adaptogenic compounds: Yellow jasmine is known for its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the nervous system. This can be especially helpful for individuals who experience chronic anxiety or stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich concentration of bioactive compounds, the roots are often used to create calming and relaxing extracts that help alleviate anxiety symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the yellow jessamine plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which are known for their anxiolytic properties and can be incorporated into creams to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality gelsemium sempervirens roots and dry them in the shade for at least two weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried gelsemium sempervirens roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered gelsemium sempervirens with 40 grams of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding any solids and reserving the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then whip it with a hand mixer until it becomes a light and fluffy cream that can be applied topically.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava creams helps with anxiety because they contain natural compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The topical application of kava extracts allows for rapid absorption into the skin, where it interacts with the body's endocannabinoid system to produce a sense of calmness and tranquility. As anxiety-related muscle tension and inflammation are alleviated, individuals may experience improved mood stability and reduced feelings of apprehension and worry, allowing them to better cope with daily life challenges.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones are a group of compounds found in kava root that have been shown to have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, likely due to their ability to bind to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in kava creams may help alleviate anxiety by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and modulating neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which are involved in mood regulation.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have been identified in kava creams and may contribute to their anxiolytic effects by inhibiting the breakdown of neurotransmitters such as GABA and increasing the production of serotonin and dopamine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in kavalactones, the bioactive compounds responsible for kava's anxiolytic and sedative properties, making them a popular choice for creating calming creams.
Rhyzomes: Containing a higher concentration of kavalactones than roots, rhyzomes are often used to create potent topical creams that target anxiety symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried kava root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the kava powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat at 70°c for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 5ml of beeswax and 5ml of shea butter, stirring until melted and blended for 15 minutes.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and mix well before pouring the mixture into a sterilized glass jar for cooling and solidification over 1 hour.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for at least 2 hours before applying topically as needed, up to 3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with anxiety because they contain the herb angelica sinensis, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. The cream's active ingredients work to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as racing thoughts and restlessness. Additionally, angelica's sedative properties can help individuals fall asleep more easily, leading to improved overall mental health and reduced anxiety levels.
remedy_properties: Cholinergic effect: Angelica's cholinergic property helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress by increasing the production of neurotransmitters like serotonin and GABA, which regulate mood.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Angelica's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to anxiety disorders, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe irritability and calmness-promoting effects.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Angelica contains flavonoids and terpenes that have a sedative and anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce anxiety symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing muscle tension, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the angelica plant are used to create calming and soothing creams due to their high concentration of phytosterols, which help to reduce stress and anxiety.
Barks: The barks of the angelica plant contain beta-amyrin acetate, a compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic effects and is often used in traditional medicine for its calming properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves by gently digging up plants in the fall or early spring.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours or air drying for 2 weeks.
Grind dried angelica archangelica into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve 500 grams.
Combine powdered angelica archangelica with 250 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating mixture to 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool until solidified, and whip it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes, resulting in a soothing anxiety-reducing cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with anxiety because of its unique ability to calm the mind and body. The apigenin and luteolin present in chamomile interact with GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress and worry. The topical application of chamomile creams allows for direct absorption into the skin, where it can quickly take effect, providing a sense of calmness and serenity. This natural approach to anxiety relief offers a gentle and soothing alternative to pharmaceuticals, making it an attractive option for those seeking a more holistic approach to managing their anxiety.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to anxiety by calming the nervous system and promoting a sense of relaxation.

Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: The apigenin and luteolin flavonoids in chamomile have been shown to have a sedative and anxiolytic effect on the nervous system, helping to calm anxious thoughts and emotions.

GABA-mimetic properties: Chamomile's GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid)-mimetic effects help increase the activity of GABA receptors in the brain, which can lead to a sense of relaxation, reduced anxiety, and improved sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which are known to have anxiolytic properties, making them effective in soothing nervous tension.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, particularly bisabolol and farnesene, which have been shown to possess calming and sedative effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with air circulation for 7-10 days or use a dehydrator.
Combine 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and gentle heating at 65-70°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and let cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with anxiety because they contain natural compounds that interact with serotonin levels in the brain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. The herbal extracts in these creams have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, such as restlessness and irritability. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients are absorbed directly into the skin, allowing for rapid absorption and effective relief from anxiety symptoms. This natural approach can be a gentle and soothing way to manage anxious feelings throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to possess anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects by modulating GABA receptors, which can help regulate stress response and promote relaxation.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in chaste tree creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety such as restlessness and irritability by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have been found to possess a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety symptoms by regulating neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in iridoid glycosides, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe anxiety-related stress.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by autoclaving them at 121 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of dried vitex agnus castus leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heatproof mixing bowl.
Place the bowl in a double boiler filled with boiling water and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and reserving the infused oil.

Whip 200 milliliters of organic coconut cream with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth consistency, then blend the whipped coconut cream with 20 grams of the infused vitex agnus castus oil and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with anxiety because they contain soothing botanicals that calm the mind and body. The natural ingredients, such as lavender and chamomile, promote relaxation and reduce feelings of stress and overwhelm. As a result, herbal marshmallow creams can help ease anxious thoughts and emotions, allowing individuals to feel more centered and at peace. Regular use can also promote improved sleep quality and a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside found in marshmallows, have been shown to have anxiolytic effects by reducing stress and anxiety responses, possibly due to their ability to interact with neurotransmitters like GABA.

Vitamins and Minerals: Marshmallow creams are rich in vitamins A, C, E, and K, as well as minerals like potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which can help alleviate anxiety symptoms by regulating the body's stress response and promoting overall relaxation.

Mucilage: The mucilage content of marshmallow creams has a soothing effect on the nervous system, providing a sense of calmness and reducing anxiety by coating the digestive tract and preventing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, the roots help to soothe and calm the nervous system, making them effective in reducing anxiety symptoms.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate anxiety-related stress and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Dry the harvested root material in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Grind dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scented geranium creams helps with anxiety because the calming and soothing properties of the plant's essential oils help to regulate emotions and reduce stress levels. The aroma of geranium is known to promote relaxation, ease nervous tension, and calm the mind. The creams also contain other soothing ingredients that work together to ease anxiety, promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, and helping to balance the emotions.
remedy_properties: Linalool: a naturally occurring compound that has been shown to have anxiolytic effects by reducing stress and anxiety responses.
Geraniol: a sweet-smelling oil that has been found to have a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of anxiety.
Rosmarinic acid: a potent antioxidant that can help reduce inflammation and stress in the body, which is often a contributing factor to anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calming compounds, the roots of scented geranium are used to make creams that promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of scented geranium contain sesquiterpenes, which help to calm the mind and body, making them ideal for anxiety relief.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250g of distilled water and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Boil 250ml of distilled water and add 10g of dried pelargonium flowers for 10 minutes of infusion time.
Combine 30g of beeswax, 20g of shea butter, and 50g of coconut oil in a separate bowl.
Blend the infused water mixture with the beeswax mixture at 60-70°c for 5-7 minutes of mixing time.
Pour the blended mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool and set for 30-40 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for anxiety are topical products containing natural ingredients from plants, herbs, and essential oils that calm the mind and body. They work by providing a sense of relaxation and tranquility, reducing feelings of stress and panic. Examples of herbal creams include those with chamomile, lavender, and lemon balm, which have been shown to soothe anxiety symptoms. Using these creams can improve lives by helping individuals relax and sleep better, reducing muscle tension, and increasing overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat anxiety with herbal creams is to choose products containing calming botanicals like lavender, chamomile, and passionflower. These herbs have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of unease and promoting relaxation. Look for creams that combine these herbs with other natural ingredients like aloe vera or jojoba oil for added skin benefits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to anxiety that are treated with herbal creams are stress, insomnia, and restlessness. Herbal creams containing ingredients like chamomile, lavender, and valerian root have been used to promote relaxation, calmness, and improve sleep quality. These natural remedies can help soothe nervous tension, reduce irritability, and promote a sense of well-being, making them an effective alternative or supplement to traditional anxiety treatments.
